faculty: Joint programme of the Faculty of Mathematics, Physics and Natural Sciences and the Faculty of Pharmacy and Medicine
application_platform: Sapienza selection registration (Infostud) plus CISIA (English TOLC-F) test registration; MoveIN for non-EU residing abroad [uncertain]
places_and_foreign_contingent: Locally programmed number of places (numero programmato locale); 50 places for 2025/2026. Non-EU contingent and 2026/2027 figure [uncertain].

academic_requirements.secondary_school_diploma: A secondary-school diploma giving access to higher education is required.
academic_requirements.test_requirement: Admission by selection based on the English TOLC-F. English level is assessed implicitly through the English TOLC test, so a separate B2 certificate is not strictly required to register for the test.
academic_requirements.non_eu_requirements: Non-EU applicants residing abroad must meet the Sapienza academic requirements (MoveIN 2026-2027) and obtain a study visa. [uncertain]

country_specific_eligibility: Country-specific qualification and minimum schooling requirements per the Sapienza academic requirements document (MoveIN 2026-2027). [uncertain]

language_requirements.level: Programme entirely in English, including the admission test. Minimum B2 (CEFR) English expected.
language_requirements.assessment: English level is assessed implicitly by completing the English TOLC-F; a B2 certificate is not required to register for the test.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: Where a certificate is required (e.g. non-EU/MoveIN route): FCE, IELTS (academic), TOEFL iBT, CAE, CPE, PTE (B2 or higher), or a university online interview. [uncertain]

language_submission_timing: Assessed via the English TOLC-F at admission; certificate route applies for non-EU/MoveIN by enrolment. [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu: EU and equivalent candidates (no visa required): on or before 31 July 2026; test/registration must meet the selection deadlines. [uncertain on exact dates]
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU residing abroad (visa-required): MoveIN online pre-selection by 15 May 2026; Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 June 2026. [uncertain on exact dates]
application_fee: €30 per application (standard Sapienza fee; max 2 applications per intake). [uncertain]
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 June 2026 for non-EU visa-required applicants.
required_documents: Secondary-school diploma / qualification giving access to higher education
English TOLC-F score (English level assessed via the test)
For non-EU residing abroad: documents required by MoveIN pre-selection and Universitaly pre-enrolment (visa)
document_translation_authentication: Foreign qualifications typically require Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability plus translation/legalisation. [uncertain]

entry_exam_or_test.note: The admission test is in English; English proficiency is verified implicitly by the English TOLC-F.

test_variant_and_session: English TOLC-F; specific 2026-2027 session dates and windows [uncertain].
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Ranking-based selection on English TOLC-F score; admission cutoff depends on ranking. [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: The BCs in Bioinformatics is entirely in English, including the admission test. Your English level will be assessed implicitly by accomplishing the English TOLC test.
The number of places available for the 2025/2026 academic year is 50 ... admission is via a locally programmed number of places (numero programmato locale) through a selective test (English TOLC-F).
Bioinformatics ... is classified as bachelor degree class L-2 ... offered through the Faculty of Pharmacy and Medicine.

uncertainty_notes: Programme URL confirmed via English-filtered catalogue: corsidilaurea.uniroma1.it/en/course/33455.
Number of places (50) is the 2025/2026 figure from the faculty FAQ; 2026/2027 figure not yet published/extracted.
Faculty is a joint programme (Mathematics/Physics/Natural Sciences + Pharmacy and Medicine); catalogue presentation primarily lists Pharmacy and Medicine.
Non-EU reserved quota, application rounds, and exact selection/test session dates not extracted from the programme Call PDF.
MoveIN requirement, application fee (€30), and document authentication inferred from general Sapienza policy.
